output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? The 5-Day Email Sequence to Get It Back
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Use this 5-day email sequence and free demand letter template to recover your money fast.
content_outline: How to Know If Your Landlord Is Illegally Withholding Your Deposit (State Deadlines & Deduction Rules)
The 5-Day Security Deposit Recovery Email Sequence: Subject Lines and Body Copy for Every Stage
When Email Isn't Enough: Escalating to a Formal Demand Letter or Small Claims Court
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days and provide an itemized list of any deductions.
Normal wear and tear cannot be legally deducted; only actual damage beyond ordinary use and unpaid rent are valid charges.
A documented email sequence creates a paper trail, but a state-compliant demand letter often triggers immediate payment.
DepositBack generates attorney-reviewed demand letters tailored to your state’s landlord-tenant laws in under 5 minutes.
output.cta: Stop waiting. Generate your free, state-specific security deposit demand letter now and force your landlord to pay what they owe.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware U.S. renters whose landlords are withholding, keeping, or failing to return security deposits.
